data.content:
The build confirms: the `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` to `obj/`, but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. SDK-style project globs expand at **evaluation time** (before targets run), so the file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. Since SDK-style projects resolve their `Compile` items via globs at evaluation time (before any targets run), the generated file isn't included in compilation.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group within the target, and also register it in `<FileWrites>` for clean support.

Let me use the skill for this since it's about including generated files.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's what was wrong and the fix:

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes a `.cs` file into `obj/` at build time, but SDK-style project globs expand at **evaluation time** (before any targets run). The generated file didn't exist yet during evaluation, so it was never added to the `Compile` item group.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="..."/>` and `<FileWrites Include="..."/>` inside the target's `<ItemGroup>` so the file is explicitly included in compilation and registered for cleanup by the `Clean` target.
